name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram at 9200 Old Cedar Ave S, Bloomington, MN 55425 in the East Bloomington neighborhood is open 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM daily. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.3 stars across 101 reviews. Call +1 952-886-0604.
description_long: MoneyGram operates as a consumer financial services company focused on money movement and cash-based transactions. The company partners with retail locations—such as the Cub Foods-Valley West location documented here—to provide accessible service points rather than operating standalone storefronts. This retail partnership model allows MoneyGram to embed its services into existing high-traffic grocery and convenience store locations. At this Bloomington, Minnesota location, MoneyGram offers core money transfer services and cash-related financial services. The company emphasizes ease of use for both sending and receiving money, with options to manage transfers online or in-person at physical locations. Services are available through extended hours of operation (open from 12:00 AM to 11:59 PM daily at this location), suggesting alignment with the host store's hours. MoneyGram's distribution model through established retail partners differentiates it from traditional check-cashing storefronts. By operating inside grocery chains and similar retailers, the company provides financial services to consumers already making regular shopping trips. The website emphasizes a multi-channel approach, allowing customers to initiate and manage transfers both digitally and offline. The company operates with standard consumer financial service requirements, including mandatory legal photo identification for cash-related transactions. The location-specific data confirms operational details but lacks deeper insight into competitive positioning or full service scope.
address: 9200 Old Cedar Ave S, Bloomington, MN 55425

company_info.headquarters: 9200 Old Cedar Ave S, Bloomington, MN 55425

pros: Extended hours of operation (open 24 hours daily at this location) provide flexibility for urgent money transfers
Convenient retail partnership location at Cub Foods eliminates need to visit standalone financial services stores
Omnichannel service model allows customers to manage transfers online or in-person
Multiple locations across the US increase accessibility for money sending and receiving
Ability to send and receive money suggests domestic and potentially international transfer capability
Simple process emphasized on website reduces friction for first-time users
cons: No fee information available on the website; MoneyGram money transfer services typically carry fees that vary by transfer amount and destination
Limited service details provided; website does not specify which money transfer corridors are available or transfer speed options
Dependent on host retail location hours for in-person service, creating operational inflexibility if Cub Foods location changes
best_for: Consumers needing to send or receive domestic money transfers quickly at a convenient retail location
People already shopping at Cub Foods who want to handle money transfers during their store visit
Individuals without traditional bank accounts who need accessible cash services
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers seeking accessible money transfer services in retail convenience settings, particularly those making regular grocery store trips. The primary caveat is that specific fees, transfer limits, and the full range of available services are not detailed on the location-specific webpage, requiring direct contact or website navigation to compare costs against competitors.
